title: Scorecard
description: One scored run: a (strategy, fixture, repeat) tuple scored on every axis. The battery runner emits one per run; the leaderboard aggregates them.

variant.description: the swept identity of this run: `${strategy}@${model}` for generative methods (so e.g. swarm@haiku and swarm@sonnet are distinct), or just the strategy name for pure-code controls (which ignore the model knob and run once). The leaderboard groups by this.

fixtureHash.description: sha256(plan.lock.json) — runs only compare within a hash

thin.description: true iff the fixture lock is THIN (lock has no `features` map ⇒ lock ⊊ manifest). On a thin fixture the GENERATIVE leap is the test, so the leaderboard's fidelity composite leans on generativeCoverage instead of build-completeness (whose planKey matching cannot see invented beads).

generativeCoverage.description: thin-input coverage: mechanical STATED-outcome coverage + bounded-judgment LATENT requirement/edge coverage. The axis that measures the GENERATIVE leap (CHARTER §8) build-completeness's planKey matching cannot see.

requirementCoverage.description: SUFFICIENCY fraction of LATENT requirements judged specified well enough to build (bounded judgment)

edgeCoverage.description: SUFFICIENCY fraction of LATENT required edges judged realized well enough to deliver (bounded judgment)

outcomeCoverage.description: fraction of STATED outcomes carried by a bead's provenance.outcomeIds (mechanical)

presenceRequirement.description: PRESENCE fraction of LATENT requirements for which a packet of the RIGHT SCOPE exists, regardless of how complete its acceptance criteria are. >= requirementCoverage (sufficiency) — the gap is the present-but-thin set.

presenceEdge.description: PRESENCE fraction of LATENT required edges for which a realized dependency edge of the right scope exists. >= edgeCoverage (sufficiency).

presenceOverall.description: combined PRESENCE generative-coverage score (pooled reqs+edges). >= overall (sufficiency); the GAP between them is the calibration finding this phase reports.

graderCost.description: What GRADING this run cost (the generative-coverage judge's calls), deltaed per run. APPARATUS cost — kept strictly separate from `cost` (the method's own spend) so grader spend can never inflate or hide inside a method's cost-effectiveness axis. Absent for runs scored without a metered judge.
